title: Effective Approaches to Attention-based Neural Machine Translation
summary: Proposed and compared two classes of attention mechanisms for neural machine translation: global attention (attending to all source words) and local attention (attending to a subset of source words). Introduced the general, dot-product, and concat scoring functions for computing attention weights. The dot-product attention variant became the basis for the scaled dot-product attention in the Transformer.
key_contributions: Introduced global and local attention mechanisms as systematic attention variants
Proposed multiple attention scoring functions including dot-product attention
Dot-product attention became the foundation of the Transformer's attention mechanism
Showed that attention mechanisms could be simplified while maintaining or improving performance
impact: This paper systematized attention mechanisms and introduced the dot-product attention scoring function that would later become the core operation of the Transformer. It bridged the gap between Bahdanau's original attention and the self-attention mechanism that powers all modern LLMs.
query_hooks: What types of attention mechanisms exist?
Where did dot-product attention come from?
How did attention mechanisms evolve before Transformers?
